QF.id: VI Public Offering and Corporate Actions_17

question.text: In case of sub-division/consolidation of shares, when is the corporate action processed?

answer.text: On the record date with credit being effected at the start of the next business day
On the record date with immediate credit
Two days after the record date
One week after the record date

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In case of sub-division/consolidation, corporate action will be processed on the record date as per the applicable ratio with credit being effected at the start of the next business day. **The Trap:** Immediate credit on the record date seems logical, but credit is at the start of the next business day. **Cross-Exam Context:** Stock split/consolidation timelines are tested in NISM Series VI. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Holdings will appear in the old ISIN until the next business day — don't panic if the balance looks different on record date evening.
